decision: When no X-Forwarded-User header is present, requireAuth/requireAuthOrThrow treat the request as a trusted local operator unless DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH=1 forces strict 401 enforcement.
rationale: Testcontainers (and other guarded routes) returned 401 in host dev mode and proxy-less Tailscale container runs because only the Pocket ID proxy injects the header; operator approved a default-bypass gate so the app is usable without a proxy while remaining lockable behind one.
alternatives: Bypass only when NODE_ENV!=production (rejected: Tailscale container is a production build with no proxy, so it would still 401); always require auth (rejected: blocks local/proxy-less use).

decision: Add requireAuth to the four mutating testcontainers compose routes (POST create, DELETE, start, stop); leave GET list unauthenticated like other read-only GETs.
rationale: Compose mutation routes previously had no auth guard at all, inconsistent with the per-container routes.
alternatives: Leave compose routes open (rejected: inconsistent and unguarded mutation).
